name: NATS.io
what_it_does: Lightweight open-source publish-subscribe messaging system with optional JetStream persistence layer for at-least-once delivery and replay.
use_cases: Fan out agent task results to multiple downstream consumers using core pub/sub subjects
Build durable work queues with JetStream consumer groups for reliable agent task distribution
Implement request-reply RPC between microservices with built-in inbox subject patterns
Stream sensor or telemetry data to multiple agent subscribers with microsecond latency
Replay historical messages from JetStream streams to backfill agent state after restart
not_for: Long-term event sourcing with years of retention — NATS storage is not optimized for that scale
Complex stream processing with joins and aggregations — use Kafka Streams or Flink instead
Teams that need a fully managed cloud service with SLA guarantees out of the box
best_when: You need extremely low latency messaging with minimal operational overhead and Kafka's JVM footprint is not acceptable.
avoid_when: Your use case requires the Kafka ecosystem (connectors, KSQL, extensive schema management) or a fully managed SaaS offering.

auth.notes: NKey-based decentralized auth is recommended; operator/account/user JWT hierarchy provides fine-grained subject-level permissions; credentials file bundles NKey + JWT for SDK use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is free; Synadia Cloud (managed) has a free tier with limited connections and storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Synadia Cloud paid plans from ~$29/month; NGS (global messaging network) usage-based pricing
pricing.notes: Core NATS server is Apache 2.0 open source; Synadia offers managed hosting separately.

performance.rate_limits: No enforced rate limits in open-source server; configurable per-account message rate limits in JWT auth; server throughput limited by hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK errors are well-typed in Go and Node; Python SDK errors can be generic; server-side errors surface as connection drops rather than descriptive messages in some cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: JetStream supports exactly-once via message deduplication window (configurable); core NATS is fire-and-forget with no delivery guarantees.
known_agent_gotchas: Core NATS has no persistence — if no subscriber is listening when a message is published it is silently dropped; agents must use JetStream for reliable delivery
Subject hierarchy uses dots as separators and wildcards (* and >) — agents that construct subject names from user input must sanitize dots and special characters
JetStream consumer acknowledge timeout defaults to 30s — long-running agent tasks must send in-progress acks or messages will be redelivered
Connection auto-reconnect is client-side — agents must handle reconnect events and re-subscribe as subscriptions are not automatically restored on all SDKs
NKey credential files contain the private signing key — logging the credentials object in debug output will expose the secret

security_score_components.security_notes: NKey cryptography is strong; TLS is opt-in on self-hosted deployments meaning misconfigured servers can run plaintext; subject-level permissions in JWT are granular and well-designed.
